texto: So he’d done more of the same. He’d drunk to forget. He’d brawled to let off steam. He’d taken the dangerous jobs to fund his lifestyle – and then began it all again. He wasn’t some chivalrous nomad, skulking from planet to planet doing good deeds and leaving when things got too hot. No, left when the bar-owner’s daughter suddenly wanted to marry him. Kanan didn’t leave because the Empire moved in: He’d stared down Imperials like Vidian before and lived. They knew he was something to ignore. No, he left because where the Empire went, fun usually died.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a John Jackson Miller: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “So he’d done more of the same. He’d drunk to forget. He’d brawled to let off steam. He’d taken th…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, empire, fun pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de casamento e fun.

texto: Kanan thumped his chest. “I’m a man with a mission.”“You’re an oaf with a delusion.
